meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.
sourceDocuments: scratch/bubbletea-v2-migration.md — Original v2-first migration plan (7 phases, zero shims mandate)
scratch/migration-guide.md — Official Bubble Tea v2 upgrade guide
scratch/migration-attempt-autopsy/ — Adversarial analysis of partial migration (8 documents + README)

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest). Use `runtime.GOOS == "windows"` and `runtime.GOOS != "windows"` skips where needed. Never accept failing or flaky tests.

GNU Make is the build system. Use `make test`, `make lint`, `make build`. On macOS use `gmake` or confirm `make` is GNU Make. Single-package tests: `go test ./internal/builtin/bubbletea/... -run TestName -v`.
Tests must not mutate host system state. Use t.TempDir(), t.Setenv(), and mock external services. Never assume a specific home directory.

ZERO COMPATIBILITY SHIMS: The migration plan explicitly BANS accepting v1 JS options (altScreen, mouse in tea.run()), keeping old command types, maintaining old message shapes alongside new ones, or any translation layer that makes v2 look like v1. If a shim exists, it must be removed — not preserved. THERE IS NO TRANSITIONAL PERIOD. Every task that introduces a v2 behavior MUST simultaneously remove the v1 equivalent. The legacy 'Mouse' case and 'Mouse' message type do not 'still work for now' — they are deleted in the same diff that adds MouseClick/MouseRelease/MouseMotion/MouseWheel.

MIGRATION PLAN IS AUTHORITATIVE: Where blueprint.json and the code disagree, the code wins. Where the code and scratch/bubbletea-v2-migration.md disagree, the migration plan wins — the code must be changed to match the plan.

BRIDGE SYMMETRY: msgToJS and JsToTeaMsg MUST agree on event shapes. If msgToJS emits MouseClick, JsToTeaMsg MUST accept MouseClick. If msgToJS emits {shiftedCode, baseCode, isRepeat}, those fields must exist in the output. Asymmetric bridges are defective by definition.

statusSection.currentState: MIGRATION SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLETE. Bubble Tea v2 migration committed (commit 36a87104): tea.View object parsing in viewDirect(), msgToJS with shiftedCode/baseCode/isRepeat, key.Text override removed, JsToTeaMsg with MouseClick/MouseRelease/MouseMotion/MouseWheel (legacy Mouse deleted), JSToMouseEvent and isWheel deleted, docs/scripting.md fixed (tea.run non-blocking), all pr_split JS migrated to v2 contract, all example scripts migrated (graphical-todo, bt-shooter, pick-and-place, minimal-bubbletea-test, benchmark-input-latency), super_document_script.js migrated, v2-native tests added, legacy test payloads deleted. TestPickAndPlaceMouseInteraction now passes: actor moves to clicked target. Remaining: bubblezone v1 dependency elimination, final multi-platform verification.
statusSection.goalState: COMPLETE Bubble Tea v2 migration with ZERO compatibility shims and ZERO transitional periods. JS view() returns structured objects mapping to tea.View — terminal features come ONLY from the view() return value. tea.run() accepts ONLY toggleKey/onToggle — all terminal feature options are DELETED (altScreen, mouse, reportFocus, windowTitle parsed from tea.run() options do not exist). JsToTeaMsg accepts ONLY v2 split mouse types (MouseClick/MouseRelease/MouseMotion/MouseWheel) — the legacy 'Mouse' case and all legacy mouse shapes are DELETED and return nil. msgToJS emits ALL documented key fields (shiftedCode, baseCode, isRepeat). Key contract is v2-native (space → 'space', not ' ') — key.Text override is DELETED. bubblezone v1 dependency is ELIMINATED. ALL scripts use v2 message shapes exclusively. ALL tests validate v2 payloads exclusively — legacy mouse payloads are invalid input that returns nil. Package docs and docs/scripting.md accurately describe the actual v2 API. make test && make lint pass on all 3 platforms.
replanLog: 2026-04-08: FULL REPLAN. Previous blueprint discarded as forensic evidence per autopsy recommendation (scratch/migration-attempt-autopsy/08_honest_conclusions.md §5). New blueprint rebuilt from original plan (scratch/bubbletea-v2-migration.md), official guide (scratch/migration-guide.md), and autopsy gap analysis (04_gap_analysis.md GAP-001 through GAP-007). Work completed in prior sessions (Go dep migration, tea.View wiring, imperative export removal, docs partial update) is preserved as Done tasks. All shim-related work is now tracked for removal.

2026-04-08: REALIGNMENT. Removed all implied legacy retention from blueprint. Task 5 (JsToTeaMsg) rewritten: legacy 'Mouse' case is DELETED simultaneously with v2 types added — no 'still works for now' language. Task 22 removed (it only existed to delete the shim later). Task 2 made explicit that all three removals (option parsing + struct fields + View() field-setting) happen in same diff. Task 20 rewritten: legacy test payloads are DELETED not updated. Task 23 (bubblezone) made explicit that deprecation-with-comments is not an option. goalState updated with 'ZERO transitional periods' and 'ONLY' language throughout. New mandatoryDirectives.noShimPeriods added. globalAlert updated with explicit 'no transitional period' and 'same diff' language.

2026-04-08: MIGRATION COMPLETE. Committed 22-file diff (36a87104): tea.View object parsing, msgToJS full key fields, key.Text override removed, JsToTeaMsg v2 split mouse types (legacy Mouse deleted), parsemouse cleanup, docs/scripting.md fix, all pr_split and example scripts migrated, v2-native tests added, legacy tests deleted. TestPickAndPlaceMouseInteraction now passes — WaitForActorPosition correctly parses [TICK-M] entries from slog log as fallback when [VIEW] is throttled.

2026-04-08: POST-MIGRATION BUG FIX (commit 2bbc93dc). ParseKey("space") returned k.Text="" for named keys, so textarea.Update fell back to key.Rune() which for String()="space" returns 's' instead of ' '. Fixed parsekey.go to set k.Text to the actual character for printable key codes (0x20-0x7E). Fixed super_document_script.js handleKeys to prefer msg.text over msg.key for printable input. TestSuperDocument now passes (10 tests, 198s). Remaining: bubblezone v1 dependency, multi-platform final verification.

2026-04-08: POST-MIGRATION BUG FIX (commit 2bbc93dc). ParseKey("space") returned k.Text="" for named keys, so textarea.Update fell back to key.Rune() which for String()="space" returns 's' instead of ' '. Fixed parsekey.go to set k.Text to the actual character for printable key codes (0x20-0x7E). Fixed super_document_script.js handleKeys to prefer msg.text over msg.key for printable input. TestSuperDocument now passes (10 tests, 198s). Remaining: bubblezone v1 dependency, multi-platform final verification.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are grouped to be executed in manageable units. Each task covers one cohesive change with its tests. Do not combine multiple unrelated fixes into one task. Read the relevant source files before each task — do not assume code state from memory.
mandatoryDirectives.tddWorkflow: For EVERY defect: (1) Write failing test(s) that reproduce the defect, (2) Verify tests fail, (3) Implement the fix, (4) Verify tests pass, (5) Run full suite `make test && make lint` before marking Done.
mandatoryDirectives.shimProhibition: If any task introduces or preserves a compatibility shim (mapping v1 JS input to v2 Go behavior, keeping old field names alongside new ones, or accepting old message shapes alongside new ones), STOP and replan. Shims are architecturally banned per scratch/bubbletea-v2-migration.md.
mandatoryDirectives.noShimPeriods: There is NO transitional period where both v1 and v2 behaviors coexist. When a task introduces a v2 behavior, it MUST simultaneously delete the v1 equivalent in the same diff. Phrases like 'still works for now', 'gated on migration', 'will be removed later', or 'legacy case still accepted' indicate a shim period and are forbidden. If a task cannot simultaneously delete v1 while adding v2, the task must be split so the v1 deletion happens first.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement JS view() returning structured objects → tea.View (core fields only)
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-001 (CRITICAL). Current: viewDirect() (bubbletea.go:857) does `viewStr := result.String()`, so JS objects become '[object Object]'. Required: When JS view() returns an object, parse it into a tea.View with support for the CORE fields only: content, altScreen, mouseMode, reportFocus, windowTitle. When it returns a string, treat as content-only (tea.NewView(str)). Additional fields (cursor, foregroundColor, backgroundColor, disableBracketedPasteMode, keyboardEnhancements, progressBar, onMouse) are deferred to future stub tasks. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go viewDirect() lines ~842-861, View() lines ~830-839.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A JS test that returns `{content: 'hello', altScreen: true, mouseMode: 'all'}` from view() produces a tea.View with AltScreen=true and MouseMode=MouseModeAllMotion. Returning a plain string still works as content-only. No existing tests break. The JS view() contract matches the plan's Section 3 item 3 (bubbletea-v2-migration.md lines ~23-24). Future stub tasks can extend this parser with additional fields.

sequentialTasks.task: Remove terminal feature option parsing from tea.run() and delete corresponding jsModel fields
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-001 (CRITICAL). Current: tea.run() parses altScreen/mouse/mouseCellMotion/reportFocus/windowTitle at bubbletea.go:1614-1628 and stores them on jsModel. This is the shim the plan bans. Remove the option parsing code, remove the altScreen/mouseMode/reportFocus/windowTitle fields from jsModel struct (lines ~520-526), remove the field-setting in View() (lines ~833-838). ALL IN THE SAME DIFF — no separate steps, no 'later' language. The ONLY options tea.run() accepts going forward are toggleKey and onToggle (lines ~1631-1650) — those are passthrough passthroughs and are not terminal features. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go lines 508-526 (struct), 830-839 (View), 1614-1628 (run options).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: tea.run(model, {altScreen:true}) is a no-op — altScreen is ignored. The ONLY way to set AltScreen is via view() returning {content:'...', altScreen:true}. tea.run(model, {toggleKey:29, onToggle:fn}) still works. jsModel struct has no altScreen/mouseMode/reportFocus/windowTitle fields. All three removals happen in the same diff. make test && make lint pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Make msgToJS emit all documented key fields: shiftedCode, baseCode, isRepeat
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-004 / GAP-007. docs/scripting.md:352 promises `{type:'Key', key, text, mod, code, shiftedCode, baseCode, isRepeat}`. msgToJS at bubbletea.go:885-891 emits only type/key/text/mod/code. Add shiftedCode (key.ShiftedCode), baseCode (key.BaseCode), isRepeat (key.IsRepeat) to the output map for both KeyPressMsg and KeyReleaseMsg cases. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go lines 885-905.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: msgToJS for KeyPressMsg returns a map containing shiftedCode, baseCode, and isRepeat keys with correct values. KeyReleaseMsg likewise. docs/scripting.md:352 field list now matches what msgToJS actually emits. Existing key tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Remove key.Text override — use msg.String() for v2-native key names
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-004. Current: bubbletea.go:874-879 overrides keyStr with key.Text when non-empty, so space remains ' ' instead of 'space'. The plan says ' ' → 'space' (msg.String()). Remove the override so that msg.String() is always used for the 'key' field. This is a BREAKING CHANGE for any JS checking msg.key === ' ' — those scripts will be migrated in later tasks. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go lines 873-879.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: msgToJS sets key=msg.String() unconditionally. Space bar produces msg.key='space' not ' '. Printable characters like 'a' still produce 'a' (msg.String() returns 'a' for printable chars). The text field still provides the actual character via key.Text.

sequentialTasks.task: Replace JsToTeaMsg legacy 'Mouse' case with v2 split mouse types (MouseClick, MouseRelease, MouseMotion, MouseWheel) — legacy case DELETED simultaneously
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-003 (HIGH). Current: JsToTeaMsg at bubbletea.go:458 only handles case 'Mouse' with legacy x/y/button/action/alt/ctrl/shift fields. This is the legacy shim. REQUIRED: DELETE the 'Mouse' case entirely. REPLACE it with four new cases: 'MouseClick', 'MouseRelease', 'MouseMotion', 'MouseWheel'. Each case constructs the corresponding tea.MouseClickMsg/tea.MouseReleaseMsg/tea.MouseMotionMsg/tea.MouseWheelMsg. Use a new v2-native helper function in parsemouse.go that accepts mod array ([]string) instead of individual alt/ctrl/shift bools — the existing JSToMouseEvent is v1 and must not be used for the new cases. Also DELETE the isWheel field from msgToJS mouse output (parsemouse.go:42) — v2 uses type-based dispatch (MouseWheel type), not action-based. Also DELETE the legacy JSToMouseEvent helper from parsemouse.go once the new v2 helper replaces it entirely. The only valid mouse input is now v2 split types. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go lines 458-500, parsemouse.go lines 42,49+.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JsToTeaMsg returns nil for {type:'Mouse', ...} objects — they are invalid input and the case does not exist. JsToTeaMsg(runtime, {type:'MouseClick', x:5, y:10, button:'left', mod:[]}) returns a non-nil tea.MouseClickMsg with correct coordinates. Same for MouseRelease, MouseMotion, MouseWheel. No isWheel field in msgToJS output. parsemouse.go has no v1-style JSToMouseEvent function. Wrapper components (viewport.go:392, textarea.go:1377) updated to expect v2 mouse types. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Update package-level Go documentation in bubbletea.go
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-007. Current: bubbletea.go:3 says 'github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea' (v1 path). Lines 52-55 document msg.runes, msg.alt, msg.ctrl, msg.paste — all removed in v2. Line 58 documents msg.action for mouse — v2 has split types. Rewrite the package comment to: (1) reference charm.land/bubbletea/v2, (2) document v2 key fields (key, text, mod, code, shiftedCode, baseCode, isRepeat), (3) document v2 mouse types (MouseClick, MouseRelease, MouseMotion, MouseWheel), (4) document v2 paste (Paste, PasteStart, PasteEnd), (5) document view() returning string or object, (6) remove all references to msg.runes, msg.alt, msg.ctrl, msg.paste, msg.action. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go lines 1-70.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Package comment references charm.land/bubbletea/v2. Zero references to msg.runes, msg.alt, msg.ctrl, msg.paste, msg.action in the package doc block. Key fields documented match what msgToJS emits. Mouse types documented match v2 split types. View contract documents both string and object return.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix docs/scripting.md — tea.run() lifecycle and key field accuracy
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-007. (1) docs/scripting.md:336 says 'blocks until exit' — bubbletea.go:1670 says 'NON-BLOCKING', returns immediately. Fix to say 'runs asynchronously' or equivalent. (2) Verify key field list at line 352 matches msgToJS output after task 3 adds shiftedCode/baseCode/isRepeat. (3) Remove tea.run() terminal option docs (altScreen, mouse, etc.) after task 2 removes them — terminal features come from view() return object. Files: docs/scripting.md lines 330-365.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: docs/scripting.md accurately describes tea.run() as non-blocking (returns immediately, program runs in background). Key field list exactly matches msgToJS output. tea.run() options section documents only toggleKey/onToggle. Terminal features documented as view() return object properties.

sequentialTasks.task: Migrate pr_split_16e_tui_update.js to v2 mouse and key contract
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-002 (CRITICAL). File: internal/command/pr_split_16e_tui_update.js. This file handles TUI update logic for the pr_split wizard. It still uses msg.type === 'Mouse' (lines ~223,236,904,926,944,971,982) and likely msg.alt/msg.ctrl/msg.action for mouse modifiers. After tasks 3-4 (v2 key fields, key.Text override removed): update msg.key === ' ' → msg.key === 'space'. After task 5 (v2 mouse input): update msg.type === 'Mouse' → msg.type === 'MouseClick' (and MouseWheel, etc.), msg.alt → msg.mod.includes('alt'), msg.ctrl → msg.mod.includes('ctrl'), msg.action → removed (type determines action).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: pr_split_16e_tui_update.js contains zero references to msg.type === 'Mouse', msg.alt, msg.ctrl, msg.action, msg.isWheel, or msg.key === ' '. All mouse handling uses MouseClick/MouseWheel/MouseMotion/MouseRelease types. All modifier checks use msg.mod array. Space bar check uses 'space'. File still passes its associated tests.

sequentialTasks.acceptance: pr_split_16a_tui_focus.js contains zero references to msg.type === 'Mouse', msg.alt, msg.ctrl, msg.action, msg.isWheel, or msg.key === ' '. All mouse handling uses v2 split types. All modifier checks use msg.mod. Associated tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Eliminate bubblezone v1 dependency — v2-native zone bounds checking or removal
sequentialTasks.description: GAP-006. bubblezone (internal/builtin/bubblezone/bubblezone.go:141-146) imports github.com/lrstanley/bubblezone which drags in github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea (v1) and github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss (v1) via go.mod. This is a v1 dependency and must be eliminated. There is no 'keep with deprecation comments' option. Options: (1) Check if lrstanley/bubblezone has a v2-compatible release and migrate to it. (2) Replace entirely with in-repo zone bounds checking — the workaround at lines 141-146 already does direct bounds checking, make that the only implementation. (3) If bubblezone is not used by any surviving code, delete the entire internal/builtin/bubblezone/ package. Files: internal/builtin/bubblezone/bubblezone.go, go.mod.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: go mod why -m github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea returns empty (no v1 reference from bubblezone). go mod why -m github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss returns empty (no v1 reference from bubblezone). internal/builtin/bubblezone/ either uses a v2-compatible zone library or implements zone bounds natively without the v1 bubblezone import. All bubblezone functionality preserved or explicitly removed. All tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Final verification: make test && make lint on macOS, Linux (Docker), Windows
sequentialTasks.description: After all prior tasks complete. Run full test suite and lint on all three platforms: macOS (local, `make test && make lint`), Linux (Docker container via `make-all-in-container` target in config.mk), and Windows (via `make-all-run-windows` using hack/run-on-windows.sh). All 30+ packages must pass on all platforms — the critical ones are internal/builtin/bubbletea (core bridge), internal/builtin/bubbles/{textarea,viewport} (wrapper components), internal/builtin/lipgloss, internal/builtin/bubblezone, internal/command (super_document, pr_split tests), and internal/scripting. Zero tolerance for failures, flakes, or race conditions. Files: entire project.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: make test exits 0 on macOS, Linux, and Windows. make lint exits 0. All packages pass. No test is skipped except those with documented GOOS guards for intentional platform differences.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: tea.View Cursor field support in JS view() return
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. The v2 tea.View supports Cursor (position, shape, color, blink). After the core migration, expose this via the JS view() return object: {content:'...', cursor: {x:5, y:10, shape:'block', color:'#fff', blink:true}}. Map to tea.Cursor struct. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go viewDirect() after task 1.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS can control cursor appearance via view() return object. cursor.x/y position the cursor. cursor.shape sets block/line/hidden. cursor.blink enables blinking. Integration test verifies escape sequences.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: tea.View ForegroundColor/BackgroundColor support in JS view() return
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. v2 tea.View supports ForegroundColor and BackgroundColor terminal-wide colors. Expose via JS: {content:'...', foregroundColor:'#ffffff', backgroundColor:'#000000'}. Map to lipgloss color types. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go viewDirect().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS can set terminal-wide foreground/background color via view() return object. Colors are applied via ANSI escape sequences. Integration test verifies output.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: tea.View KeyboardEnhancements support
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. v2 tea.View supports KeyboardEnhancements for progressive enhancement of keyboard protocol (Kitty keyboard protocol). Expose via JS: {content:'...', keyboardEnhancements: {...}}. Research the exact v2 API shape before implementing. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go viewDirect().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS can request keyboard enhancements via view() return object. key.ShiftedCode and key.BaseCode are populated when enhancements are active. Integration test verifies behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: tea.View OnMouse intercept support
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. v2 tea.View supports OnMouse for intercepting mouse messages based on view content (for zone-based click handling). Expose via JS if/when bubblezone is replaced with v2-native zone support. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go, possibly internal/builtin/bubblezone/.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS can define mouse intercept zones via view() return object or equivalent API. Click handling is zone-aware without external bubblezone dependency. Tests verify zone hit detection.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: tea.View ProgressBar support
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. v2 tea.View supports ProgressBar for native terminal progress bars. Expose via JS: {content:'...', progressBar: {current:50, total:100, width:40}}. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go viewDirect().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS can display a terminal progress bar via view() return object. Progress bar renders in terminal title bar or equivalent location. Integration test verifies output.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: tea.View DisableBracketedPasteMode support
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. v2 tea.View supports DisableBracketedPasteMode. Expose via JS: {content:'...', disableBracketedPaste: true}. Currently bracketed paste is always on. This allows disabling it per-view. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go viewDirect().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS can disable bracketed paste via view() return object. When disabled, paste events come as regular Key events instead of Paste messages. Test verifies behavior change.

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: Performance benchmarks for v2 migration
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. After migration is complete, benchmark the v2 bridge performance: (1) message throughput (msgToJS/JsToTeaMsg round-trip), (2) view() parsing overhead (string vs object), (3) render latency comparison with pre-migration baseline. Create benchmark targets in Makefile. Files: internal/builtin/bubbletea/benchmark_latency_test.go, config.mk.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Benchmark suite runs via make benchmark. Results documented in scratch/. No performance regression vs pre-migration baseline on key metrics (message round-trip, render latency).

sequentialTasks.task: STUB: Interactive end-to-end validation of all migrated scripts
sequentialTasks.description: FUTURE EXPANSION. After all migration and test tasks are complete, manually or semi-automatically validate each migrated script works correctly in a real terminal: (1) example-02-graphical-todo.js — alt screen, mouse interaction, space key, (2) example-04-bt-shooter.js — gameplay with keyboard and mouse, (3) example-05-pick-and-place.js — drag and drop with mouse, (4) pr_split wizard — full TUI workflow with mouse. Document results in scratch/.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Each script is verified working in a real terminal on at least one platform. Alt screen activates correctly. Mouse events are received and processed. Space key works as 'space'. Paste works for textarea content. Results documented in scratch/e2e-validation.md.

continuousVerification.task: Run project-defined build and test commands after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute `make test` and `make lint` after each task completion. Specifically: (1) After view() object parsing — run bubbletea tests and verify escape sequences; (2) After tea.run() option removal — verify options are ignored; (3) After JsToTeaMsg v2 mouse — verify wrapper tests pass; (4) After each script migration — verify associated tests pass; (5) After all fixes — full `make test && make lint` on all platforms.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: 100% unit test coverage of changed code paths. Integration tests required for: view() object parsing, msgToJS key field emission, JsToTeaMsg v2 mouse types, tea.run() option rejection.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ZERO COMPATIBILITY SHIMS — tea.run() MUST NOT accept terminal feature options. JS view() MUST return structured objects for terminal control. The only way to set AltScreen/MouseMode/etc. is via view() return value, not tea.run() options.

BRIDGE SYMMETRY — msgToJS and JsToTeaMsg must agree on ALL message shapes. If msgToJS emits MouseClick, JsToTeaMsg must accept MouseClick. If msgToJS emits shiftedCode/baseCode/isRepeat, those fields must be in the output. Asymmetric bridges are defective.
ALL platforms must pass. The tilde behavior differs between POSIX and Windows by design. Every test touching tilde expansion MUST have appropriate GOOS guards. Never remove a GOOS skip.
